title: Delegation Modes
description: Delegation can start from a clean room or inherit the parent context.

steps.content: Investigate the parser bug, and keep my earlier naming decisions in mind.
steps.annotation: The task may or may not need prior context.

steps.content: {"mode":"fresh","task":"Inspect parser.py and report the likely bug."}
steps.annotation: Fresh delegation isolates the child from the parent transcript.

steps.content: Fresh worker summary: parser.py assumes comma-separated input and crashes on tabs.
steps.annotation: A clean-room worker returns only the useful summary.

steps.content: {"mode":"fork","task":"Patch the parser bug while preserving the naming conventions we already chose."}
steps.annotation: Forked delegation inherits the parent conversation and its decisions.

steps.content: Forked worker summary: parser now accepts tabs and kept the established token names.
steps.annotation: Inheritance matters when the child must respect prior context.

steps.content: Fresh delegation was enough for diagnosis; forked delegation was better for context-sensitive implementation.
steps.annotation: The chapter teaches delegation strategy, not just delegation existence.
